children.heading: Tariff classification
children.label: 1 Tariff classification
children.text: The basis for any tariff classification is the HTS.

children.heading: Reference to HTS
children.label: 2 Reference to HTS
children.text: Whenever in this section there is a reference to a chapter, heading, or subheading, such reference shall be a reference to a chapter, heading, or subheading of the HTS.

children.heading: Cost or value
children.label: 3 Cost or value
children.text: Any cost or value referred to in this section shall be recorded and maintained in accordance with the generally accepted accounting principles applicable in the territory of the country in which the good is produced (whether the United States or another CAFTA–DR country).
the good is a good wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries;

children.label: i
children.text: each of the nonoriginating materials used in the production of the good undergoes an applicable change in tariff classification specified in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the good otherwise satisfies any applicable regional value-content or other requirements specified in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement; and

children.label: B
children.text: satisfies all other applicable requirements of this section; or
the good is produced entirely in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries, exclusively from materials described in paragraph (1) or (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b)(2), the regional value-content of a good referred to in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement, except for goods to which paragraph (4) applies, shall be calculated by the importer, exporter, or producer of the good, on the basis of the build-down method described in paragraph (2) or the build-up method described in paragraph (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The regional value-content of a good may be calculated on the basis of the following build-down method:rvc=av–vnm×100av

children.heading: RVC
children.label: i RVC
children.text: The term “RVC” means the regional value-content of the good, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: AV
children.label: ii AV
children.text: The term “AV” means the adjusted value of the good.

children.heading: VNM
children.label: iii VNM
children.text: The term “VNM” means the value of nonoriginating materials that are acquired and used by the producer in the production of the good, but does not include the value of a material that is self-produced.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The regional value-content of a good may be calculated on the basis of the following build-up method:rvc=vom×100av

children.heading: RVC
children.label: i RVC
children.text: The term “RVC” means the regional value-content of the good, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: AV
children.label: ii AV
children.text: The term “AV” means the adjusted value of the good.

children.heading: VOM
children.label: iii VOM
children.text: The term “VOM” means the value of originating materials that are acquired or self-produced, and used by the producer in the production of the good.

children.heading: Special rule for certain automotive goods
children.label: 4 Special rule for certain automotive goods

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b)(2), the regional value-content of an automotive good referred to in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement may be calculated by the importer, exporter, or producer of the good, on the basis of the following net cost method:rvc=nc–vnm×100nc

children.heading: Automotive good
children.label: i Automotive good
children.text: The term “automotive good” means a good provided for in any of subheadings 8407.31 through 8407.34, subheading 8408.20, heading 8409, or in any of headings 8701 through 8708.

children.heading: RVC
children.label: ii RVC
children.text: The term “RVC” means the regional value-content of the automotive good, expressed as a percentage.

children.heading: NC
children.label: iii NC
children.text: The term “NC” means the net cost of the automotive good.

children.heading: VNM
children.label: iv VNM
children.text: The term “VNM” means the value of nonoriginating materials that are acquired and used by the producer in the production of the automotive good, but does not include the value of a material that is self-produced.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to all motor vehicles in any 1 of the categories described in clause (ii); or

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to all motor vehicles in any such category that are exported to the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries.

children.label: I
children.text: is the same model line of motor vehicles, is in the same class of vehicles, and is produced in the same plant in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country, as the good described in clause (i) for which regional value-content is being calculated;

children.label: II
children.text: is the same class of motor vehicles, and is produced in the same plant in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country, as the good described in clause (i) for which regional value-content is being calculated; or

children.label: III
children.text: is the same model line of motor vehicles produced in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country as the good described in clause (i) for which regional value-content is being calculated.

children.label: I
children.text: the fiscal year of the motor vehicle producer to whom the automotive goods are sold,

children.label: II
children.text: any quarter or month, or

children.label: III
children.text: its own fiscal year,

children.label: ii
children.text: determine the average referred to in clause (i) separately for such goods sold to 1 or more motor vehicle producers; or

children.label: iii
children.text: make a separate determination under clause (i) or (ii) for automotive goods that are exported to the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries.

children.label: i
children.text: calculating the total cost incurred with respect to all goods produced by the producer of the automotive good, subtracting any sales promotion, marketing and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and nonallowable interest costs that are included in the total cost of all such goods, and then reasonably allocating the resulting net cost of those goods to the automotive good;

children.label: ii
children.text: calculating the total cost incurred with respect to all goods produced by that producer, reasonably allocating the total cost to the automotive good, and then subtracting any sales promotion, marketing and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and nonallowable interest costs that are included in the portion of the total cost allocated to the automotive good; or

children.label: iii
children.text: reasonably allocating each cost that forms part of the total cost incurred with respect to the automotive good so that the aggregate of all such costs does not include any sales promotion, marketing and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, or nonallowable interest costs.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a material that is imported by the producer of the good, the adjusted value of the material;

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a material acquired in the territory in which the good is produced, the value, determined in accordance with Articles 1 through 8, Article 15, and the corresponding interpretive notes of the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade 1994 referred to in , as set forth in regulations promulgated by the Secretary of the Treasury providing for the application of such Articles in the absence of an importation; orsection 3511(d)(8) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: all expenses incurred in the production of the material, including general expenses; and

children.label: ii
children.text: an amount for profit equivalent to the profit added in the normal course of trade.

children.heading: Further adjustments to the value of materials
children.label: 2 Further adjustments to the value of materials
i
children.text: The costs of freight, insurance, packing, and all other costs incurred in transporting the material within or between the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries to the location of the producer.

children.label: ii
children.text: Duties, taxes, and customs brokerage fees on the material paid in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries, other than duties or taxes that are waived, refunded, refundable, or otherwise recoverable, including credit against duty or tax paid or payable.

children.label: iii
children.text: The cost of waste and spoilage resulting from the use of the material in the production of the good, less the value of renewable scrap or byproducts.

children.label: i
children.text: The costs of freight, insurance, packing, and all other costs incurred in transporting the material within or between the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries to the location of the producer.

children.label: ii
children.text: Duties, taxes, and customs brokerage fees on the material paid in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries, other than duties or taxes that are waived, refunded, refundable, or otherwise recoverable, including credit against duty or tax paid or payable.

children.label: iii
children.text: The cost of waste and spoilage resulting from the use of the material in the production of the good, less the value of renewable scrap or byproducts.

children.label: iv
children.text: The cost of originating materials used in the production of the nonoriginating material in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries.

children.heading: Originating materials used in production of goods of another country
children.label: 1 Originating materials used in production of goods of another country
children.text: Originating materials from the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries that are used in the production of a good in the territory of another CAFTA–DR country shall be considered to originate in the territory of that other country.

children.heading: Multiple procedures
children.label: 2 Multiple procedures
children.text: A good that is produced in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries by 1 or more producers is an originating good if the good satisfies the requirements of subsection (b) and all other applicable requirements of this section.

children.heading: De minimis amounts of nonoriginating materials
children.label: f De minimis amounts of nonoriginating materials
i
children.text: are used in the production of the good, and

children.label: ii
children.text: do not undergo the applicable change in tariff classification (set out in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement),

children.label: B
children.text: the good meets all other applicable requirements of this section; and

children.label: C
children.text: the value of such nonoriginating materials is included in the value of nonoriginating materials for any applicable regional value-content requirement for the good.

children.label: A
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in chapter 4, or a nonoriginating dairy preparation containing over 10 percent by weight of milk solids provided for in subheading 1901.90 or 2106.90, that is used in the production of a good provided for in chapter 4.

children.label: i
children.text: Infant preparations containing over 10 percent by weight of milk solids provided for in subheading 1901.10.

children.label: ii
children.text: Mixes and doughs, containing over 25 percent by weight of butterfat, not put up for retail sale, provided for in subheading 1901.20.

children.label: iii
children.text: Dairy preparations containing over 10 percent by weight of milk solids provided for in subheading 1901.90 or 2106.90.

children.label: iv
children.text: Goods provided for in heading 2105.

children.label: v
children.text: Beverages containing milk provided for in subheading 2202.90.

children.label: vi
children.text: Animal feeds containing over 10 percent by weight of milk solids provided for in subheading 2309.90.

children.label: C
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in heading 0805, or any of subheadings 2009.11 through 2009.39, that is used in the production of a good provided for in any of subheadings 2009.11 through 2009.39, or in fruit or vegetable juice of any single fruit or vegetable, fortified with minerals or vitamins, concentrated or unconcentrated, provided for in subheading 2106.90 or 2202.90.

children.label: D
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in heading 0901 or 2101 that is used in the production of a good provided for in heading 0901 or 2101.

children.label: E
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in heading 1006 that is used in the production of a good provided for in heading 1102 or 1103 or subheading 1904.90.

children.label: F
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in chapter 15 that is used in the production of a good provided for in chapter 15.

children.label: G
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in heading 1701 that is used in the production of a good provided for in any of headings 1701 through 1703.

children.label: H
children.text: A nonoriginating material provided for in chapter 17 that is used in the production of a good provided for in subheading 1806.10.

children.label: I
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraphs (A) through (H) and Annex 4.1 of the Agreement, a nonoriginating material used in the production of a good provided for in any of chapters 1 through 24, unless the nonoriginating material is provided for in a different subheading than the good for which origin is being determined under this section.

children.label: i
children.text: the total weight of all such fibers or yarns in that component is not more than 10 percent of the total weight of that component; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the yarns are those described in (as in effect on ).section 3203(b)(3)(B)(vi)(IV) of this titleAugust 2, 2005

children.heading: Certain textile or apparel goods
children.label: B Certain textile or apparel goods
children.text: A textile or apparel good containing elastomeric yarns in the component of the good that determines the tariff classification of the good shall be considered to be an originating good only if such yarns are wholly formed in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country.

children.heading: Yarn, fabric, or fiber
children.label: C Yarn, fabric, or fiber
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, in the case of a good that is a yarn, fabric, or fiber, the term “component of the good that determines the tariff classification of the good” means all of the fibers in the good.

children.heading: Claim for preferential tariff treatment
children.label: A Claim for preferential tariff treatment
children.text: A person claiming that a fungible good or fungible material is an originating good may base the claim either on the physical segregation of the fungible good or fungible material or by using an inventory management method with respect to the fungible good or fungible material.

children.label: i
children.text: averaging;

children.label: ii
children.text: “last-in, first-out”;

children.label: iii
children.text: “first-in, first-out”; or

children.label: I
children.text: recognized in the generally accepted accounting principles of the CAFTA–DR country in which the production is performed; or

children.label: II
children.text: otherwise accepted by that country.

children.heading: Election of inventory method
children.label: 2 Election of inventory method
children.text: A person selecting an inventory management method under paragraph (1) for a particular fungible good or fungible material shall continue to use that method for that fungible good or fungible material throughout the fiscal year of that person.

children.heading: Accessories, spare parts, or tools
children.label: h Accessories, spare parts, or tools
A
children.text: be treated as originating goods if the good is an originating good; and

children.label: B
children.text: be disregarded in determining whether all the nonoriginating materials used in the production of the good undergo the applicable change in tariff classification set out in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the accessories, spare parts, or tools are classified with and not invoiced separately from the good, regardless of whether they appear specified or separately identified in the invoice for the good; and

children.label: B
children.text: the quantities and value of the accessories, spare parts, or tools are customary for the good.

children.heading: Regional value-content
children.label: 3 Regional value-content
children.text: If the good is subject to a regional value-content requirement, the value of the accessories, spare parts, or tools shall be taken into account as originating or nonoriginating materials, as the case may be, in calculating the regional value-content of the good.

children.heading: Packaging materials and containers for retail sale
children.label: i Packaging materials and containers for retail sale
children.text: Packaging materials and containers in which a good is packaged for retail sale, if classified with the good, shall be disregarded in determining whether all the nonoriginating materials used in the production of the good undergo the applicable change in tariff classification set out in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement, and, if the good is subject to a regional value-content requirement, the value of such packaging materials and containers shall be taken into account as originating or nonoriginating materials, as the case may be, in calculating the regional value-content of the good.

children.heading: Packing materials and containers for shipment
children.label: j Packing materials and containers for shipment
children.text: Packing materials and containers for shipment shall be disregarded in determining whether a good is an originating good.

children.heading: Indirect materials
children.label: k Indirect materials
children.text: An indirect material shall be treated as an originating material without regard to where it is produced.

undergoes further production or any other operation outside the territories of the CAFTA–DR countries, other than unloading, reloading, or any other operation necessary to preserve the good in good condition or to transport the good to the territory of a CAFTA–DR country; or
does not remain under the control of customs authorities in the territory of a country other than a CAFTA–DR country.

children.heading: Goods classifiable as goods put up in sets
children.label: m Goods classifiable as goods put up in sets

children.text: each of the goods in the set is an originating good; or

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of textile or apparel goods, 10 percent of the adjusted value of the set; or

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a good, other than a textile or apparel good, 15 percent of the adjusted value of the set.

children.heading: Adjusted value
children.label: 1 Adjusted value
children.text: The term “adjusted value” means the value determined in accordance with Articles 1 through 8, Article 15, and the corresponding interpretive notes of the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade 1994 referred to in , adjusted, if necessary, to exclude any costs, charges, or expenses incurred for transportation, insurance, and related services incident to the international shipment of the merchandise from the country of exportation to the place of importation.section 3511(d)(8) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the United States; and

children.label: B
children.text: Costa Rica, the Dominican Republic, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, or Nicaragua, for such time as the Agreement is in force between the United States and that country.

children.label: A
children.text: Motor vehicles provided for in subheading 8701.20, 8704.10, 8704.22, 8704.23, 8704.32, or 8704.90, or heading 8705 or 8706, or motor vehicles for the transport of 16 or more persons provided for in subheading 8702.10 or 8702.90.

children.label: B
children.text: Motor vehicles provided for in subheading 8701.10 or any of subheadings 8701.30 through 8701.90.

children.label: C
children.text: Motor vehicles for the transport of 15 or fewer persons provided for in subheading 8702.10 or 8702.90, or motor vehicles provided for in subheading 8704.21 or 8704.31.

children.label: D
children.text: Motor vehicles provided for in any of subheadings 8703.21 through 8703.90.

children.heading: Fungible good or fungible material
children.label: 4 Fungible good or fungible material
children.text: The term “fungible good” or “fungible material” means a good or material, as the case may be, that is interchangeable with another good or material for commercial purposes and the properties of which are essentially identical to such other good or material.

children.heading: Generally accepted accounting principles
children.label: 5 Generally accepted accounting principles
children.text: The term “generally accepted accounting principles” means the recognized consensus or substantial authoritative support in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country with respect to the recording of revenues, expenses, costs, assets, and liabilities, the disclosure of information, and the preparation of financial statements. The principles may encompass broad guidelines of general application as well as detailed standards, practices, and procedures.

children.heading: Goods wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries
children.label: 6 Goods wholly obtained or produced entirely in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries
A
children.text: plants and plant products harvested or gathered in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries;

children.label: B
children.text: live animals born and raised in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries;

children.label: C
children.text: goods obtained in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries from live animals;

children.label: D
children.text: goods obtained from hunting, trapping, fishing or aquaculture conducted in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries;

children.label: E
children.text: minerals and other natural resources not included in subparagraphs (A) through (D) that are extracted or taken in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries;

children.label: F
children.text: fish, shellfish, and other marine life taken from the sea, seabed, or subsoil outside the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries by vessels registered or recorded with a CAFTA–DR country and flying the flag of that country;

children.label: G
children.text: goods produced on board factory ships from the goods referred to in subparagraph (F), if such factory ships are registered or recorded with that CAFTA–DR country and fly the flag of that country;

children.label: H
children.text: goods taken by a CAFTA–DR country or a person of a CAFTA–DR country from the seabed or subsoil outside territorial waters, if a CAFTA–DR country has rights to exploit such seabed or subsoil;

children.label: I
children.text: goods taken from outer space, if the goods are obtained by a CAFTA–DR country or a person of a CAFTA–DR country and not processed in the territory of a country other than a CAFTA–DR country;

children.label: i
children.text: manufacturing or processing operations in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries; or

children.label: ii
children.text: used goods collected in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries, if such goods are fit only for the recovery of raw materials;

children.label: K
children.text: recovered goods derived in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries from used goods, and used in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country in the production of remanufactured goods; and

children.label: i
children.text: goods referred to in any of subparagraphs (A) through (J), or

children.label: ii
children.text: the derivatives of goods referred to in clause (i),

children.heading: Identical goods
children.label: 7 Identical goods
children.text: The term “identical goods” means identical goods as defined in the Agreement on Implementation of Article VII of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade 1994 referred to in ;section 3511(d)(8) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: fuel and energy;

children.label: B
children.text: tools, dies, and molds;

children.label: C
children.text: spare parts and materials used in the maintenance of equipment or buildings;

children.label: D
children.text: lubricants, greases, compounding materials, and other materials used in production or used to operate equipment or buildings;

children.label: E
children.text: gloves, glasses, footwear, clothing, safety equipment, and supplies;

children.label: F
children.text: equipment, devices, and supplies used for testing or inspecting the good;

children.label: G
children.text: catalysts and solvents; and

children.label: H
children.text: any other goods that are not incorporated into the good but the use of which in the production of the good can reasonably be demonstrated to be a part of that production.

children.heading: Material
children.label: 9 Material
children.text: The term “material” means a good that is used in the production of another good, including a part or an ingredient.

children.heading: Material that is self-produced
children.label: 10 Material that is self-produced
children.text: The term “material that is self-produced” means an originating material that is produced by a producer of a good and used in the production of that good.

children.heading: Model line
children.label: 11 Model line
children.text: The term “model line” means a group of motor vehicles having the same platform or model name.

children.heading: Net cost
children.label: 12 Net cost
children.text: The term “net cost” means total cost minus sales promotion, marketing, and after-sales service costs, royalties, shipping and packing costs, and non-allowable interest costs that are included in the total cost.

children.heading: Nonallowable interest costs
children.label: 13 Nonallowable interest costs
children.text: The term “nonallowable interest costs” means interest costs incurred by a producer that exceed 700 basis points above the applicable official interest rate for comparable maturities of the CAFTA–DR country in which the producer is located.

children.heading: Nonoriginating good or nonoriginating material
children.label: 14 Nonoriginating good or nonoriginating material
children.text: The terms “nonoriginating good” and “nonoriginating material” mean a good or material, as the case may be, that does not qualify as originating under this section.

children.heading: Packing materials and containers for shipment
children.label: 15 Packing materials and containers for shipment
children.text: The term “packing materials and containers for shipment” means the goods used to protect a good during its transportation and does not include the packaging materials and containers in which a good is packaged for retail sale.

children.heading: Preferential tariff treatment
children.label: 16 Preferential tariff treatment
children.text: The term “preferential tariff treatment” means the customs duty rate, and the treatment under article 3.10.4 of the Agreement, that are applicable to an originating good pursuant to the Agreement.

children.heading: Producer
children.label: 17 Producer
children.text: The term “producer” means a person who engages in the production of a good in the territory of a CAFTA–DR country.

children.heading: Production
children.label: 18 Production
children.text: The term “production” means growing, mining, harvesting, fishing, raising, trapping, hunting, manufacturing, processing, assembling, or disassembling a good.

children.heading: Reasonably allocate
children.label: 19 Reasonably allocate
children.text: The term “reasonably allocate” means to apportion in a manner that would be appropriate under generally accepted accounting principles.

children.label: A
children.text: the disassembly of used goods into individual parts; and

children.label: B
children.text: the cleaning, inspecting, testing, or other processing that is necessary for improvement to sound working condition of such individual parts.

children.label: A
children.text: is entirely or partially comprised of recovered goods; and

children.label: B
children.text: has a similar life expectancy and enjoys a factory warranty similar to such a new good.

children.heading: Total cost
children.label: 22 Total cost
children.text: The term “total cost” means all product costs, period costs, and other costs for a good incurred in the territory of one or more of the CAFTA–DR countries.

children.heading: Used
children.label: 23 Used
children.text: The term “used” means used or consumed in the production of goods.

children.label: A
children.text: the provisions set out in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement; and

children.label: B
children.text: any additional subordinate category necessary to carry out this subchapter consistent with the Agreement.

children.heading: Fabrics and yarns not available in commercial quantities in the United States
children.label: 2 Fabrics and yarns not available in commercial quantities in the United States
children.text: The President is authorized to proclaim that a fabric or yarn is added to the list in Annex 3.25 of the Agreement in an unrestricted quantity, as provided in article 3.25.4(e) of the Agreement.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to the consultation and layover provisions of , the President may proclaim modifications to the provisions proclaimed under the authority of paragraph (1)(A), other than provisions of chapters 50 through 63, as included in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement.section 4014 of this title

children.heading: Additional proclamations
children.label: B Additional proclamations
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), and subject to the consultation and layover provisions of , the President may proclaim before the end of the 1-year period beginning on , modifications to correct any typographical, clerical, or other nonsubstantive technical error regarding the provisions of chapters 50 through 63, as included in Annex 4.1 of the Agreement.section 4014 of this titleAugust 2, 2005

children.heading: Fabrics, yarns, or fibers not available in commercial quantities in the CAFTA–DR countries
children.label: 4 Fabrics, yarns, or fibers not available in commercial quantities in the CAFTA–DR countries

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph 3(A), the list of fabrics, yarns, and fibers set out in Annex 3.25 of the Agreement may be modified as provided for in this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: The term “interested entity” means the government of a CAFTA–DR country other than the United States, a potential or actual purchaser of a textile or apparel good, or a potential or actual supplier of a textile or apparel good.

children.label: ii
children.text: All references to “day” and “days” exclude Saturdays, Sundays, and legal holidays.

children.heading: Requests to add fabrics, yarns, or fibers
children.label: C Requests to add fabrics, yarns, or fibers
i
children.text: An interested entity may request the President to determine that a fabric, yarn, or fiber is not available in commercial quantities in a timely manner in the CAFTA–DR countries and to add that fabric, yarn, or fiber to the list in Annex 3.25 of the Agreement in a restricted or unrestricted quantity.

children.label: I
children.text: the fabric, yarn, or fiber is available in commercial quantities in a timely manner in the CAFTA–DR countries; or

children.label: II
children.text: any interested entity objects to the request.

children.label: I
children.text: the fabric, yarn, or fiber is not available in commercial quantities in a timely manner in the CAFTA–DR countries; or

children.label: II
children.text: no interested entity has objected to the request.

children.label: I
children.text: not later than 30 days after the date on which the request is submitted under clause (i); or

children.label: II
children.text: not later than 44 days after the request is submitted, if the President determines, within 30 days after the date on which the request is submitted, that the President does not have sufficient information to make a determination under clause (ii).

children.label: v
children.text: Notwithstanding , a proclamation made under clause (iii) shall take effect on the date on which the text of the proclamation is published in the Federal Register.section 4013(a)(2) of this title

children.label: vi
children.text: Not later than 6 months after proclaiming under clause (iii) that a fabric, yarn, or fiber is added to the list in Annex 3.25 of the Agreement in a restricted quantity, the President may eliminate the restriction if the President determines that the fabric, yarn, or fiber is not available in commercial quantities in a timely manner in the CAFTA–DR countries.

children.label: i
children.text: 45 days after the date on which the request was submitted; or

children.label: ii
children.text: 60 days after the date on which the request was submitted, if the President made a determination under subparagraph (C)(iv)(II).

children.heading: Requests to restrict or remove fabrics, yarns, or fibers
children.label: E Requests to restrict or remove fabrics, yarns, or fibers
I
children.text: that has been added to that list in an unrestricted quantity pursuant to paragraph (2) or subparagraph (C)(iii) or (D); or

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to which the President has eliminated a restriction under subparagraph (C)(vi).

children.label: ii
children.text: An interested entity may submit a request under clause (i) at any time beginning 6 months after the date of the action described in subclause (I) or (II) of that clause.

children.label: iii
children.text: Not later than 30 days after the date on which a request under clause (i) is submitted, the President may proclaim an action provided for under clause (i) if the President determines that the fabric, yarn, or fiber that is the subject of the request is available in commercial quantities in a timely manner in the CAFTA–DR countries.

children.label: iv
children.text: A proclamation declared under clause (iii) shall take effect no earlier than the date that is 6 months after the date on which the text of the proclamation is published in the Federal Register.

children.label: i
children.text: governing the submission of a request under subparagraphs (C) and (E); and

children.label: ii
children.text: providing an opportunity for interested entities to submit comments and supporting evidence before the President makes a determination under subparagraph (C)(ii) or (vi) or (E)(iii).
